response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals and exceeds the experience requirement (19 years), they are fundamentally a web and backend developer with minimal C++ expertise. The role requires deep C++ and systems programming skills as its core responsibility, along with industrial automation and IoT domain knowledge—areas where the candidate has little to no background. The career path divergence is too significant, and bringing them up to speed in embedded systems development, real-time programming, and industrial protocols would require extensive retraining.
strengths: Proven mentoring and leadership experience across multiple roles, with workshops and community contributions demonstrating ability to share knowledge effectively
Strong software engineering fundamentals including TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and code quality practices from senior-level positions
19 years of professional experience exceeding the 8-12 year requirement, with demonstrated ability to learn diverse technology stacks
concerns: Minimal C++ experience (only ~6 months at National Instruments and minor hobby projects), which contradicts the core requirement for architecting high-performance C++ applications with STL
No evidence of systems programming, embedded systems, IoT, or industrial automation background—all critical for this automation machinery role
No demonstrated knowledge of industrial protocols (MQTT, Modbus, IEC standards), secure development lifecycle (IEC 62443), or real-time multi-threaded systems, which are central responsibilities

key_factors.factor: C++ and systems programming expertise gap
Missing industrial automation and IoT domain knowledge
Senior-level experience and software craftsmanship fundamentals
